message.content:
I need to build endpoint-to-scope mappings for 5 remaining APIs. For each, I need a JSON file mapping REST API endpoints to OAuth scopes. This is for a proxy-based policy enforcement system.

Output format:
```json
{
  "provider": "mailchimp",
  "apiHost": "*.api.mailchimp.com",
  "basePath": "/3.0",
  "scopeMap": [
    {
      "method": "GET|POST|PATCH|DELETE",
      "pathPattern": "/3.0/...",
      "sufficientScopes": ["lists.read"],
      "description": "..."
    }
  ],
  "allScopes": [...]
}
```

Use `*` for variable path segments (IDs).

## 1. Mailchimp (*.api.mailchimp.com/3.0)
Mailchimp doesn't have granular OAuth scopes — it's all-or-nothing access. Define semantic scopes for policy enforcement:
- lists.read, lists.write, campaigns.read, campaigns.write, templates.read, templates.write, automations.read, automations.write, reports.read, members.read, members.write, segments.read, segments.write, files.read, files.write, accounts.read

Endpoints:
- Lists/audiences (list, get, create, update, delete)
- Members (list, get, create/add, update, delete/archive, tags)
- Segments (list, get, create, update, delete, members)
- Campaigns (list, get, create, update, delete, send, schedule, cancel, replicate, content, feedback)
- Templates (list, get, create, update, delete)
- Automations (list, get, start, pause, emails, queue)
- Reports (list, get, campaign reports, click/open reports)
- Landing pages
- File manager (list, get, upload, delete)
- Merge fields
- Batch operations
- Account info / root

Base path: /3.0

## 2. Figma (api.figma.com)
Figma OAuth scopes: files:read, file_variables:read, file_variables:write, file_comments:write, file_dev_resources:read, file_dev_resources:write, library_analytics:read, webhooks:write, org:read, org:manage_members

Endpoints:
- Files (get, get images, get image fills, get versions)
- File nodes (get specific nodes)
- File comments (list, create, delete, get reactions, create reaction, delete reaction)
- File variables (get local, get published, create/update/delete)
- File dev resources (get, create, update, delete)
- Components (get, list team components, list file components)
- Component sets (get, list)
- Styles (get, list team styles, list file styles)
- Projects (list files in project)
- Team (get team projects)
- Users (get me)
- Webhooks (list, get, create, update, delete)
- Activity/version history
- Library analytics
- Payments

Base path: /v1

## 3. Calendly (api.calendly.com)
Calendly OAuth scopes: default (full access). Define semantic scopes:
- events.read, events.write, users.read, scheduling_links.read, scheduling_links.write, organizations.read, organizations.write, webhooks.read, webhooks.write, availability.read, availability.write, invitees.read

Endpoints:
- Scheduled events (list, get, cancel)
- Event invitees (list, get)
- Event types (list, get)
- Users (get current, get by URI)
- Organization memberships (list, get, delete)
- Organization invitations (list, get, create, revoke)
- Scheduling links (create)
- Webhook subscriptions (list, get, create, delete)
- Availability schedules (list, get user availability)
- User busy times
- Activity log entries

Base path: none (root-level paths, e.g., /scheduled_events)

## 4. Typeform (api.typeform.com)
Typeform OAuth scopes: forms:read, forms:write, images:read, images:write, themes:read, themes:write, responses:read, responses:write, workspaces:read, workspaces:write, webhooks:read, webhooks:write, accounts:read, offline:read, offline:write

Endpoints:
- Forms (list, get, create, update, delete, get messages)
- Responses (list, delete)
- Insights (get summary)
- Images (list, get, create, delete)
- Themes (list, get, create, update, delete)
- Workspaces (list, get, create, update, delete, add/remove members)
- Webhooks (get, create, update, delete)
- Accounts (get)

Base path: none or /forms, etc.

## 5. Zoom (api.zoom.us/v2)
Zoom OAuth scopes are granular. Key scopes:
- meeting:read, meeting:write, meeting:master, webinar:read, webinar:write, user:read, user:write, recording:read, recording:write, phone:read, phone:write, dashboard:read, report:read, group:read, group:write, account:read, account:write, chat_message:read, chat_message:write, chat_channel:read, chat_channel:write, contact:read, contact:write

Endpoints:
- Meetings (list, get, create, update, delete, update status, list registrants, add registrant, list polls, create poll)
- Meeting participants (list)
- Meeting recordings (list, get, delete, recover)
- Users (list, get, create, update, delete, update status, get settings, update settings)
- Webinars (list, get, create, update, delete, registrants, polls, panelists)
- Cloud recordings (list, get, delete)
- Reports (daily, meeting participants, webinar, etc.)
- Dashboard (meetings, webinars, CRC, IM)
- Groups (list, get, create, update, delete, members)
- Roles (list, get, members)
- Chat channels (list, get, create, update, delete, members)
- Chat messages (list, send, update, delete)
- Contacts (list, get)
- Phone (if relevant)

Base path: /v2

Write:
- /workspace/scope-maps/mailchimp.json
- /workspace/scope-maps/figma.json
- /workspace/scope-maps/calendly.json
- /workspace/scope-maps/typeform.json
- /workspace/scope-maps/zoom.json

Search the web for each provider's API reference. Be thorough!

Here's a summary of what I found about the **Calendly API v2 REST Endpoints Reference** for 2025:

## Overview

API v2 is Calendly's current, actively maintained API. The Calendly API v2 is REST-based and has predictable resource-oriented URLs. It uses JSON for request and response bodies and standard HTTP methods, authentication, and response codes.

## Key Endpoints

Based on the developer documentation and guides, here are some of the main REST endpoints available:

- **Users:** `GET /users/me` — You can make a call to the Get User API endpoint or Get Current User endpoint to receive the user value on the URI and the organization value.
- **Event Types:** `GET /event_types` — Retrieve event types filtered by user or organization.
- **Scheduled Events:** `GET /scheduled_events` — List scheduled events.
- **Cancel Event:** `POST /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/cancellation` — To cancel an event via the API, you can send a POST request to the cancel event endpoint.
- **Available Times:** `GET /event_type_available_times` — Check available times for a given event type.
- **Busy Times:** `GET /user_busy_times` — Retrieve a user's busy times.
- **Organization Memberships:** `GET /organization_memberships` — You can make a GET request to the List Organization Memberships endpoint which returns a collection of all the Calendly members.
- **Webhooks:** An administrator can delete organization-scoped webhooks created by another administrator using the Delete Webhook Subscription endpoint.

## New: Scheduling API (Create Event Invitee)

The Scheduling API makes it possible to create new Calendly events programmatically. Using the Create Event Invitee endpoint, your users can book meetings on behalf of their invitees directly from your apps and workflows — no redirects, iframes, or Calendly UI required. Calendly users must be on a paid plan to access or use applications with the Scheduling API.

## Access & Pricing

Developers can make GET and POST requests to API endpoints on behalf of a Calendly user on any subscription plan, including the Basic plan. However, certain endpoints are available only to the Enterprise plan. For webhooks and Scheduling API endpoints, the Calendly user must have a paid subscription on the Professional, Standard, Teams, or Enterprise plan.

## Migration from v1

Support for V1 API and Webhooks will begin phasing out in May 2025. While still supported until then, Calendly does not recommend using API v1, webhooks created using API v1, or apps integrated using the API key on your integrations page.

## Official Documentation

- **API Reference:** [developer.calendly.com/api-docs](https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs)
- **Getting Started:** [developer.calendly.com/getting-started](https://developer.calendly.com/getting-started)
- **Migration Guide:** [developer.calendly.com/how-to-migrate-from-api-v1-to-api-v2](https://developer.calendly.com/how-to-migrate-from-api-v1-to-api-v2)
- **Release Notes:** [developer.calendly.com/release-notes](https://developer.calendly.com/release-notes)
- Everything you need to get started with Calendly APIs is in the Developer Portal, where you'll find reference docs, guides, and sample code to help you start building.

Here's a summary of the Zoom REST API v2 endpoints reference based on the search results:

## Zoom REST API v2 — Endpoints Reference

### Official Documentation

Zoom offers a wide variety of API endpoints and events to power your applications and integrations. The primary official references are:

- **API Reference (Main):** [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/)
- **Zoom Meeting API Methods:** [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/)
- **Zoom Developer APIs Index:** [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/index/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/index/)

### Overview

The Zoom API provides the ability for developers to easily add Video, Voice and Screen Sharing to your application. Their API is a server-side implementation designed around REST, and it does not support cross-origin resource sharing or access via a client-side web application.

The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics. It does not provide access to the in-meeting experience such as current attendee list, who is the current speaker, or ability to mute participants; for those features, you should look at Zoom's SDKs.

### Base URL

The API base URL is:
```
https://api.zoom.us/v2/
```

### Key Endpoint Categories

Based on the documentation and search results, the Zoom REST API v2 covers the following major areas:

1. **Accounts** – Create, manage, and configure sub-accounts (master/sub-account relationships).
2. **Users** – List, create, update, and delete users (e.g., `GET /v2/users`).
3. **Meetings** – Create, update, delete, and list meetings.
4. **Webinars** – Manage webinars and webinar settings.
5. **Rooms** – Manage Zoom Rooms (e.g., `POST /v2/rooms/{room_id}/meetings`).
6. **Phone** – Phone-related endpoints including call logs.
7. **Plans & Billing** – Subscribe, update, and manage account plans.
8. **Reporting & Analytics** – Access post-meeting data.
9. **Recording** – Manage cloud recordings.
10. **Settings** – Account-level and user-level settings configuration.

### Authentication

The Zoom API is RESTful, which means you access it via URL by sending it your credential token, and possibly some parameters, and getting back the information you requested. Zoom currently uses **OAuth 2.0** and **Server-to-Server OAuth** for authentication (JWT was deprecated).

### Recent Updates / Deprecations (2025)

There are ongoing deprecation changes, including the phone REST API `call_logs` endpoint. The deprecation date has been discussed with references to June 18, 2025, and November 30, 2025, depending on the changelog source.

### Developer Platform

The Zoom Developer Platform is an open platform that allows third-party developers to build applications and integrations upon Zoom's video-first unified communications platform.

### Additional Resources

- **GitHub Repository:** [github.com/zoom/api](https://github.com/zoom/api) — Zoom API Version 2 Documentation
- **Developer Forum:** [devforum.zoom.us](https://devforum.zoom.us) — For support and discussions
- **Full Developer Docs:** [developers.zoom.us/docs/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/)

For the complete and most up-to-date list of all individual endpoints, I recommend visiting the official [Zoom Meeting API Methods page](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/), which provides detailed documentation for every available endpoint.

Here's a summary of the Typeform API REST endpoints reference based on the search results:

## Typeform REST API Reference

### Overview

Typeform's APIs and developer tools let you do everything from the basics, like tweaking themes and adding new images to your account, to the most powerful features like retrieving responses on demand and customizing forms with Hidden Fields.

### Base URL

The base URL for Create, Responses, and Webhooks is `https://api.typeform.com/`. If your account is configured to store responses in the EU Data Center, the base URL you should use is `https://api.eu.typeform.com` or `https://api.typeform.eu`.

### Authentication

Typeform uses OAuth 2.0 to secure the endpoints for the Create, Responses, and Webhooks APIs. To use the APIs, you need to pass an access token in the Authorization header of your requests.

### Three Main RESTful APIs

Typeform covers three RESTful APIs: Create, Responses, and Webhooks. Create, Responses, and Webhooks use the HTTP protocols POST, PUT, GET, and DELETE for requests, and they return responses in JSON data format.

#### 1. **Create API**
Create is a RESTful API that gives you direct control over your typeforms, images, and themes, plus more customization options. The Create API involves three key concepts: forms, images, and themes.

Key endpoints include:
- `GET https://api.typeform.com/images` — retrieves a list of all the images in your account.
- `POST https://api.typeform.com/images` — adds a new image to your account.
- `GET https://api.typeform.com/themes` — retrieves available themes in your account.
- `POST https://api.typeform.com/themes` — adds a new theme.
- `PUT https://api.typeform.com/forms` — updates a form, specifying values for fields.

#### 2. **Responses API**
The response includes submissions your typeform has received historically, in JSON format. Very recent submissions (say within the last 30 minutes) may not be included in this API call.

Key endpoints include:
- `GET https://api.typeform.com/forms/{form_id}/responses` — retrieves all responses for a typeform. This endpoint is part of the Responses API.

Useful query parameters:
- `page_size` — lets you specify the number of results to retrieve (up to 1000), and `query` — lets you limit responses to those that contain a certain term.
- `since` and `until` — you can use these to specify which results you want to see, such as limiting results to a specific date.

#### 3. **Webhooks API**
Send every submission for your typeform directly to any URL or compatible web application, as soon as it's submitted.

### Other Endpoints
- `GET https://api.typeform.com/me` — retrieves your Typeform alias, email address, and language.

### Parameters

Some endpoints involve path parameters, which are part of the endpoint itself and considered a requirement to make the request. Query parameters are optional — you can use them to limit and configure the scope of your requests.

### Additional Resources

- **Official Developer Portal**: [https://www.typeform.com/developers/](https://www.typeform.com/developers/)
- **Postman Collection**: A Typeform API Reference is also available on the [Postman API Network](https://www.postman.com/typeform/typeform-public-workspace/documentation/sau1ujn/typeform-api-reference).
- All you need is a Typeform account to use the APIs — there's no special developer account.

Here's a summary of the Figma REST API endpoints reference based on the latest documentation:

## Figma REST API Endpoints Reference

### Overview

The Figma API is based on the REST structure, supporting authentication via access tokens and OAuth2. Requests are made via HTTP endpoints with clear functions and appropriate response codes.

All REST API endpoints use the same base URL: `https://api.figma.com`. For example: `GET https://api.figma.com/v1/files/:key`. For Figma for Government, the base URL is: `https://api.figma-gov.com`.

### Authentication

When you make requests to endpoints in the Figma REST API, you must authenticate the requests. You can authenticate using OAuth 2 (recommended), which lets you make requests with access tokens obtained on behalf of users, or using personal access tokens.

For both OAuth apps and personal access tokens, you'll specify what scopes you want to permit. Scopes define what resources the token can access. For example, to read file content, you would grant the `file_content:read` scope.

### File Endpoints

Files endpoints allow for a wide range of functionality to get information about files. With a specific file key, you can get the JSON and image representations of the whole file or individual nodes within the file.

Key file endpoints include:

- **GET files/:key** — Returns the document referred to by `:key` as a JSON object. The file key can be parsed from any Figma file URL. This is a Tier 1 endpoint and requires the `file_content:read` scope.

- **GET files/:key/nodes** — Returns the nodes referenced by `:ids` as a JSON object. The nodes are retrieved from the Figma file referenced by `:key`. The node ID and file key can be parsed from any Figma node URL.

- **GET image fills** — Returns a mapping from image references to downloadable URLs. Image URLs expire after no more than 14 days. Image references are located in the output of the GET files endpoint under the `imageRef` attribute in a Paint.

- **GET file metadata** — Returns the metadata for the file referred to by `:key`. This is a Tier 3 endpoint and requires the `file_metadata:read` scope.

### Recent Changes & Updates (2025)

New scopes have been added for the REST API. The `files:read` scope is no longer recommended and will be deprecated. Instead, use more specific scopes like `file_content:read` or `file_comments:read`.

Added support for `targetAspectRatio` in the GET files endpoint, allowing proper proportional resizing on the canvas, unlike the legacy `preserveRatio` field.

New Enterprise beta endpoints have been added for reading Library Analytics — fetching action time series data and library usage grouped by different dimensions.

### Rate Limits (Updated November 2025)

As of November 17, 2025, the updated rate limits are in effect.

Rate limits apply to all users and vary depending on the API tier, the user's Figma plan (Starter, Professional, Organization, or Enterprise), and seat type (View, Collab, Dev, or Full). Personal access tokens are also subject to rate limits.

For managing rate limits, Figma uses a leaky bucket algorithm. When the REST API is unable to fulfill requests due to the bucket being full or the rate limit being exceeded, the endpoint returns a 429 error.

### OpenAPI Specification

The Figma REST API is fully described in an OpenAPI specification in the open source `figma/rest-api-spec` repository.

Types directly associated with API endpoints are prefixed with the OpenAPI operation ID (e.g., `getFile` → `GetFilePathParams`, `GetFileQueryParams`, `GetFileResponse`). For endpoints expecting a request body, types are suffixed with `RequestBody` (e.g., `postComments` → `PostCommentsRequestBody`).

### OAuth App Requirements (2025)

OAuth apps that use Figma's REST API now need to provide additional information and undergo review by the Figma team before they're published. Apps created before September 23, 2025, needed to be re-published by November 17, 2025.

For full details, visit the official documentation at [developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/).

Here's a summary of the Mailchimp API v3.0 REST endpoints reference based on the search results:

## Mailchimp Marketing API v3.0 — REST Endpoints Reference

### Overview

The Marketing API generally follows REST conventions, with some deviations. Resources are typically nouns like subscribers or campaigns, and subresources can be multiply nested under resources. Mailchimp uses the OpenAPI Specification (formerly known as Swagger) to describe each endpoint.

### Base URL

The root URL for the API is `https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/`. The `<dc>` part of the URL corresponds to the data center for your account. For example, if the data center for your account is `us6`, all API endpoints for your account are available relative to `https://us6.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/`.

### Supported HTTP Methods

The Mailchimp Marketing API accepts the standard HTTP methods: GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, and DELETE.

- Make a GET request to retrieve data. GET requests will never cause an update or change to your data because they're safe and idempotent.
- Use a POST request to create new resources. For example, make a POST request to a collection endpoint where the body of your request JSON contains all of the required information about the new object.
- Make a PATCH request to update a resource.

### Non-Standard Actions

Some endpoints act on resources outside of a traditional REST approach. To pause an automation workflow, for example, you make a POST request to `/automations/{workflow_id}/emails/{id}/actions/pause`. Where they deviate from the verb usage defined by REST, they namespace those verbs under `actions` in the URI.

### Key Endpoint Categories (from API Reference)

The official [Mailchimp Marketing API Reference](https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api/) includes these major endpoint groups:

- **Audiences (BETA):** Your Mailchimp audience is where you store and manage all of your omni-channel contacts. Endpoints include getting audiences, managing contacts (create, retrieve, update, archive, forget).
- **Authorized Apps:** Manage registered, connected apps for your Mailchimp account with the Authorized Apps endpoints.
- **Automations:** Manually add a subscriber to a workflow, bypassing the default trigger settings. You can also use this endpoint to trigger a series of automated emails in an API 3.0 workflow type.
- **Connected Sites:** Manage sites you've connected to your Mailchimp account. Get all connected sites in an account.
- **Campaigns, Reports, Lists/Members**, and more.

### API Version Notes

Mailchimp retired API Export 1.0 and API 2.0 on June 1, 2023. The previous version 2.0 has not been shut off, but is deprecated and unsupported. They recommend migrating to version 3.0 since version 2.0 is not regularly tested or maintained.

### Authentication

Your API key also contains data center info — if your API key is `0123456789abcdef0123456789abcde-us6`, then the data center subdomain is `us6`.

### Contact Identification

When making a call for information about a particular contact, the Marketing API uses the MD5 hash of the lowercase version of the contact's email address. This makes it less likely to leak email addresses — these hashes can't be translated back to an email address.

### Performance Considerations

Response times are dependent on the complexity of your request and the general load across Mailchimp. Some endpoints return values that are large and slow to calculate. Once you know what data you need, use the pagination and partial response capabilities to request only what is essential to you.

For the full official API reference, visit: [https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api/](https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api/)

# Figma File-Related REST API Endpoints

Based on the developer documentation, here are all file-related endpoints:

| Endpoint | HTTP Method | Path | Required Scope |
|----------|------------|------|----------------|
| Get File | GET | `/v1/files/:key` | `file_content:read` |
| Get File Nodes | GET | `/v1/files/:key/nodes` | `file_content:read` |
| Get Image | GET | `/v1/images/:key` | `file_content:read` |
| Get Image Fills | GET | `/v1/files/:key/images` | `file_content:read` |
| Get File Metadata | GET | `/v1/files/:key/meta` | `file_metadata:read` |

## Key Details

**GET File** retrieves "the document referred to by `:key` as a JSON object" with full tree structure and metadata.

**GET File Nodes** returns specific nodes you request via comma-separated IDs, useful for partial document retrieval.

**GET Image** renders nodes as image files in multiple formats (JPG, PNG, SVG, PDF) with customizable scaling and SVG options.

**GET Image Fills** provides "download links for all images present in image fills in a document."

**GET File Metadata** returns file-level metadata including creator, last modified user, and folder information.

All endpoints accept optional `version` parameters to retrieve historical file states. The first four are Tier 1 endpoints; metadata is Tier 3.

Here's an overview of the **Zoom API v2 REST endpoints** based on the search results:

## Zoom API v2 — REST Endpoints Overview

The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics.

The base URL is `api.zoom.us` followed by the version number of the API, currently at version 2. All requests must be sent via HTTPS; requests sent to HTTP will receive a 302 HTTP response code.

### Key API Categories

1. **Meetings API**
   - The Zoom Meeting APIs allow developers to interface with Zoom Meetings and Webinars programmatically.
   - Endpoints for creating, listing, updating, and deleting meetings.

2. **Users API**
   - The Zoom User API allows you to manage Contact Groups, Groups, and Users data for building applications on the Zoom App Marketplace with OAuth authorization.

3. **Webinars API**
   - Part of the meetings suite for managing webinar lifecycle (create, update, delete, list registrants, panelists, etc.).

4. **Recordings API**
   - Endpoints for managing cloud recordings (list, get, delete recordings for meetings).

5. **Reports API**
   - Access post-meeting data including meeting/webinar reports, participant details, and usage statistics.

6. **Dashboard API**
   - Zoom APIs enable developers to request information from Zoom, including (but not limited to) user details, meeting reports, dashboard data, as well as perform actions on the Zoom developer platform on a user's behalf.
   - Provides real-time and historical dashboard metrics.

7. **Groups API**
   - Manage groups within a Zoom account (list, create, update, delete groups and group members).

8. **Chat API**
   - Endpoints for Zoom Team Chat — manage chat channels, messages, and files.

### Additional Notes

- The API does not provide access to the in-meeting experience such as current attendee list, who is the current speaker, or ability to mute participants; for those features, refer to Zoom SDKs.
- Webhooks can be used as notifications to keep your systems in sync with actions inside Zoom.
- The Zoom Developer Platform is an open platform that allows third-party developers to build applications and integrations upon Zoom's video-first unified communications platform.

Here's an overview of the **Calendly API v2** endpoints and key resources based on the search results:

---

## Calendly API v2 — Key Endpoints

### Users
- **`GET /users/me`** — Get your own info (current authenticated user)

### Organizations
- **`GET /organization_memberships?organization={org_uri}`** — All members of an organization
- The **List Organization Memberships** endpoint returns a collection of all the Calendly members, including each user's organization URI.

### Event Types
- **`GET /event_types?user={user_uri}`** — All event types for a user
- **`GET /event_types?organization={org_uri}`** — All event types for an organization
- **`GET /event_types/{uuid}`** — Retrieve a specific event type

### Scheduled Events
- **`GET /scheduled_events`** — List scheduled events
- **`POST /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/cancellation`** — Cancel an event
- To get further details about an event invitee, the webhook payload includes the invitee URI which can be used to make a GET request to the **Get Event Invitee** endpoint.

### Scheduling (New Scheduling API)
- Calendly now enables AI assistants or agents to book meetings end-to-end via the **Scheduling API** endpoint, using the **Create Event Invitee** endpoint to programmatically schedule events.
- **`POST invitees`** — Schedule a meeting by creating an invitee for a specific event/time.

### Availability
- **`GET /event_type_available_times?event_type={uri}&start_time=...`** — Available times for an event type
- **`GET /user_busy_times?user={user_uri}&start_time=...`** — User's busy times
- **`GET /user_availability_schedules`** — Retrieve availability schedules for a specified user (pass the user URI).
- Note: `start_time` and `end_time` parameters are required, must be in the future, and cannot be a range greater than 7 days.

### Webhooks
- Make a **`POST`** request to the **`/webhook_subscriptions`** endpoint, add your personal access token to authenticate, set your server endpoint in the url value, and set `invitee.created`, `invitee.canceled`, or `routing_form_submission.created` for the events value.
- You can also **retrieve a list of all webhook subscriptions** for the authenticated user or organization.
- **Delete a specific webhook subscription** by providing the UUID of the webhook subscription you want to delete.

### Webhook Event Types
Calendly supports the following webhook subscriptions:
- **Invitee Created Events** — when a new event is scheduled
- **Invitee Canceled Events** — when an existing event is canceled
- **Routing Form Submissions** — when someone submits a routing form (with or without booking)

---

## Important Notes

- Webhooks need at least a **Standard plan** ($10/user/month), and enterprise-specific endpoints require Enterprise plans.
- Calendly is **deprecating API v1 starting August 2025**.
- A new **Scheduling API** is now available that lets you build scheduling directly into your app without redirects, iframes, or Calendly-hosted UI.
- The Scheduling API functionality requires an **OAuth application** to leverage the API on behalf of end-users.

For the full interactive API reference, visit the [Calendly Developer Portal](https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs).

Here's an overview of the **Mailchimp Marketing API v3.0** and its key endpoint categories:

## Overview

The Mailchimp Marketing API provides programmatic access to Mailchimp data and functionality, allowing developers to build custom features to do things like sync email activity and campaign analytics with their database, manage audiences and campaigns, and more.

The root URL for the API is `https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/`. The `<dc>` part of the URL corresponds to the data center for your account.

The Mailchimp Marketing API accepts the standard HTTP methods: GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, and DELETE.

---

## Key Endpoint Categories

### 1. **Lists/Audiences & Members**
- Batch subscribe or unsubscribe list members.
- Manage abuse complaints for a specific list. An abuse complaint occurs when your recipient reports an email as spam in their mail program.
- Get recent daily, aggregated activity stats for your list. For example, view unsubscribes, signups, total emails sent, opens, clicks, and more, for up to 180 days.
- Get information about the most popular email clients for subscribers in a specific Mailchimp list.
- Use the Events endpoint to collect website or in-app actions and trigger targeted automations.
- View a summary of the month-by-month growth activity for a specific list. Get a month-by-month summary of a specific list's growth activity.
- Resource path example: `https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/lists/{list_id}/members/{subscriber_hash}/notes/{id}`.
- When making a call for information about a particular contact, the Marketing API uses the MD5 hash of the lowercase version of the contact's email address.

### 2. **Campaigns**
- Manage the HTML, plain-text, and template content for your Mailchimp campaigns.
- Post comments, reply to team feedback, and send test emails while you're working together on a Mailchimp campaign.
- Review the send checklist for your campaign, and resolve any issues before sending.
- Remove the guesswork for resending a campaign to certain segments. You can use this endpoint as a shortcut to replicate a campaign and resend it to common segments, such as those who didn't open the campaign, or any new subscribers since it was sent.

### 3. **Automations (Classic)**
- Use the API to manage workflows, emails, and queues for Classic Automations. Does not include Automation flows. Get a summary of an account's classic automations.
- Create a new classic automation in your Mailchimp account.
- Get a summary of an individual classic automation workflow's settings and content. The trigger_settings object returns information for the first email in the workflow.
- Start all emails in a classic automation workflow.
- Pause all emails in a specific classic automation workflow.
- Archiving will permanently end your automation and keep the report data. You'll be able to replicate your archived automation, but you can't restart it.
- Manage individual emails in a classic automation workflow. Get a summary of the emails in a classic automation workflow. Get information about an individual classic automation workflow email.
- Manage list member queues for classic automation emails.
- Remove subscribers from a classic automation workflow. Remove a subscriber from a specific classic automation workflow. You can remove a subscriber at any point in an automation workflow, regardless of how many emails they've been sent from that workflow. Once they're removed, they can never be added back to the same workflow.

### 4. **Reports**
- You can find a collection of reports at the Reports endpoint, or individual reports at the `/reports/{campaign_id}` endpoint.
- Get report details for a specific sent campaign.

### 5. **E-Commerce**
- Connect your E-commerce Store to Mailchimp to take advantage of powerful reporting and personalization features and to learn more about your customers.
- Deleting a store will also delete any associated subresources, including Customers, Orders, Products, and Carts.
- Orders represent successful e-commerce transactions, and this data can be used to provide more detailed campaign reports, track sales, and personalize emails to your targeted consumers.

### 6. **Other Endpoints**
- **Chimp Chatter**: Get the latest Chimp Chatter activity from your account.
- **Conversations** (deprecated): Conversations has been deprecated in favor of Inbox and these endpoints don't include Inbox data. Past Conversations are still available via this endpoint, but new campaign replies and other Inbox messages aren't available using this endpoint.
- **Connected Sites**: Manage sites you've connected to your Mailchimp account. Get all connected sites in an account.

---

## API Technical Details

- Mailchimp uses the OpenAPI Specification (formerly known as Swagger) to describe each endpoint.
- You can paginate your API requests to limit response results and make them easier to work with by requesting manageable chunks of data. Mailchimp uses offset and count in the URL query string to paginate.
- The Marketing API has a 120-second timeout on API calls.
- Some endpoints act on resources outside of a traditional REST approach. To pause an automation workflow, for example, you make a POST request to `/automations/{workflow_id}/emails/{id}/actions/pause`.

For the full API reference, visit: [Mailchimp Marketing API Reference](https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api/)

Here's a comprehensive overview of the **Figma REST API** — its endpoint categories, scopes, and key features:

---

## Overview

The Figma API supports access and interactions with Figma's different products, giving you the ability to view and extract objects or layers and their properties from files, get usage data, or listen for events with webhooks, among other things. All REST API endpoints use the same base URL: `https://api.figma.com`.

The Figma REST API is fully described in an OpenAPI specification in the open source `figma/rest-api-spec` repository.

---

## Endpoint Categories

Endpoints allow you to perform a number of actions: Make requests for different resources — Files, Images, File versions, Users, Comments, Projects, Components and styles — Work with variables — Manage dev resources — Get usage and analytics data (Activity logs, Text events, Library analytics) — Create and manage webhooks.

Based on the OpenAPI spec and official docs, the API is organized into **14 resource groups** and **46 operations**. Here are the major categories:

### 1. **Files**
The GET file endpoint is a Tier 1 endpoint and requires the `file_content:read` scope. Key file endpoints include:
- **GET file** — Returns the full document JSON
- **GET file nodes** — Returns specific nodes by ID
- **GET images** — Renders nodes as images (PNG, SVG, JPG, PDF)
- **GET image fills** — Returns download links for all images present in image fills in a document. Image fills are how Figma represents any user-supplied images.
- **GET file metadata** — This is a Tier 3 endpoint and requires the `file_metadata:read` scope.

### 2. **Comments**
In addition to accessing files and layers, you will be able to GET and POST comments to files.
- **GET comments** — Retrieve comments on a file
- **POST comment** — Add a comment to a file
- **DELETE comment** — Remove a comment
- The `as_md` parameter was added to the GET comments endpoint to return rich-text comments as markdown.

### 3. **Components & Styles**
The components key contains a mapping from node IDs to component metadata, to help you determine which components each instance comes from.
- **GET team/file components** — List published components
- **GET component** — Get a specific component's metadata
- **GET team/file styles** — List published styles
- **GET style** — Get a specific style's metadata

### 4. **Variables** (Enterprise only)
The Variables REST API includes endpoints for querying, creating, updating, and deleting variables. Variables in Figma store reusable values that can be applied to all kinds of design properties and prototyping actions.
- **GET local variables** — Retrieve local variables from a file
- **POST variables** — Create/update variables
- **GET published variables** — Get published variables from a library
- To use this API, you must have a Full seat in an Enterprise org; guests cannot use the API.

### 5. **Dev Resources**
New endpoints for querying, creating, and updating dev resources. Dev resources are developer-contributed URLs that are attached to nodes in files and are shown in Figma Dev Mode.
- **GET dev resources** — List dev resources on a file
- **POST dev resources** — Create dev resources
- **PUT dev resources** — Update dev resources
- **DELETE dev resources** — Support for deleting dev resources was added.

### 6. **Users**
- **GET /v1/me** — Returns the email associated with the user's account. This property is only present on the `/v1/me` endpoint.

### 7. **File Versions**
- **GET file versions** — Retrieve version history of a file

### 8. **Projects**
- **GET team projects** — List projects in a team
- **GET project files** — List files in a project
- Approval is now required to use the Projects endpoints. If you're building a new app and would like to use these endpoints, you can request access.

### 9. **Webhooks**
Changes to Webhooks V2 enable additional ways to utilize webhooks. In addition to teams, you can now attach webhooks to specific files and projects. When you create a webhook, you now specify a context (team, file, or project) and the ID of the context.
- **POST webhook** — This is a Tier 2 endpoint and requires the `webhooks:write` scope.
- **GET webhook** — This is a Tier 2 endpoint and requires the `webhooks:read` scope.
- **GET webhooks** — Returns a list of webhooks corresponding to the context or plan provided, if they exist. For plan, the webhooks for all contexts that you have access to will be returned, and the response is paginated.
- **PUT webhook** — Update a webhook
- **DELETE webhook** — Deletes the specified webhook. This operation cannot be reversed.
- **GET webhook requests** — Returns all webhook requests sent within the last week. Useful for debugging.

### 10. **Analytics / Library Analytics** (Enterprise)
New Enterprise beta endpoints for reading Library Analytics. Fetch action time series data and library usage grouped by different dimensions.

### 11. **Activity Logs** (Enterprise)
- **GET activity logs** — Get activity logs as an organization admin

### 12. **Payments**
Introduced the Payments REST API and GET payments endpoint.

---

## Authentication & Scopes

When you make requests to endpoints in the Figma REST API, you must authenticate the requests. You can authenticate in two ways: Create an OAuth app to use OAuth 2, which lets you make requests with access tokens obtained on behalf of users.

Scopes for personal access tokens and OAuth 2 tokens determine which endpoints can be accessed. The following table lists the scopes that are available.

Key scopes include:

| Scope | Description |
|---|---|
| `file_content:read` | Read file content |
| `file_metadata:read` | Read file metadata |
| `file_comments:read` | Read comments |
| `file_comments:write` | Write comments |
| `file_variables:read` | Read variables |
| `file_variables:write` | Write variables |
| `file_dev_resources:read` | Read dev resources |
| `file_dev_resources:write` | Write dev resources |
| `webhooks:read` | Read webhooks |
| `webhooks:write` | Create/update/delete webhooks |
| `current_user:read` | Read current user info |

Added new scopes for the REST API. The `files:read` scope is no longer recommended and will be deprecated in the future. Instead of `files:read`, use a more specific scope like `file_content:read` or `file_comments:read`.

The `file_read` scope was deprecated because it gave access to too many endpoints. There are more granular scopes now to allow you to create tokens with more fine-grained access.

---

## OpenAPI Specification

Types directly associated with API endpoints are prefixed with the OpenAPI operation ID (e.g. `getFile` → `GetFilePathParams`, `GetFileQueryParams`, `GetFileResponse`). For API endpoints expecting a request body, the types are suffixed with `RequestBody` (e.g. `postComments` → `PostCommentsRequestBody`).

The full specification is available at: **[github.com/figma/rest-api-spec](https://github.com/figma/rest-api-spec)**

---

## Recent Changes

- New requirements for publishing OAuth apps, and updated rate limits for the REST API went into effect on November 17, 2025.
- Apps using the `file_read` and `files:read` scopes today need to switch to new, granular scopes like `file_content:read` and `file_metadata:read` for more precise permissions.

For the full, authoritative list of every endpoint with request/response details, visit the official documentation at **[developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/](https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/)**.

Here's a comprehensive summary of the Mailchimp API 3.0 OpenAPI/Swagger specification and where to find all endpoint paths:

## Mailchimp Marketing API 3.0 — OpenAPI/Swagger Specification

### Official Swagger/OpenAPI Spec File

The official Mailchimp Marketing API OpenAPI specification is maintained as part of the tool used to auto-generate and publish the Mailchimp Marketing and Transactional client libraries, located at `mailchimp-client-lib-codegen/spec/marketing.json` on GitHub.

You can find the full spec file here:
- **Marketing API spec**: [https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-client-lib-codegen/blob/main/spec/marketing.json](https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-client-lib-codegen/blob/main/spec/marketing.json)
- **Transactional API spec**: [https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-client-lib-codegen/blob/main/spec/transactional.json](https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-client-lib-codegen/blob/main/spec/transactional.json)

### API Base URL

The root URL for the API is `https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/`. The `<dc>` part of the URL corresponds to the data center for your account. For example, if the data center for your account is `us6`, all API endpoints are available relative to `https://us6.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/`.

### OpenAPI Specification Format

Mailchimp uses the OpenAPI Specification (formerly known as Swagger) to describe each endpoint. The spec was updated to be OAS 2-compliant, making it possible to directly use the Swagger file in Postman and integrate with many other OAS-friendly tools.

### Key API Endpoint Categories (from the API Reference)

Based on the [Mailchimp Marketing API Reference](https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/api/), the major endpoint groups include:

| Resource | Example Path |
|---|---|
| **Account Exports** | `/account-exports` |
| **Audiences (Lists)** | `/lists`, `/lists/{list_id}` |
| **Audience Contacts (Members)** | `/lists/{list_id}/members` |
| **Batch Operations** | `/batches` |
| **Batch Webhooks** | `/batch-webhooks` |
| **Campaign Folders** | `/campaign-folders` |
| **Campaigns** | `/campaigns`, `/campaigns/{campaign_id}` |
| **Campaign Content** | `/campaigns/{campaign_id}/content` |
| **Connected Sites** | `/connected-sites` |
| **Conversations** | `/conversations` |
| **E-commerce Stores** | `/ecommerce/stores` |
| **File Manager** | `/file-manager/files`, `/file-manager/folders` |
| **Landing Pages** | `/landing-pages` |
| **Reports** | `/reports` |
| **Search** | `/search-campaigns`, `/search-members` |
| **Segments** | `/lists/{list_id}/segments` |
| **Tags** | `/lists/{list_id}/tag-search` |
| **Templates** | `/templates` |
| **Template Folders** | `/template-folders` |
| **Automations** | `/automations` |
| **Ping** | `/ping` |
| **Root** | `/` |

### Codegen and Client Libraries

Mailchimp uses a Swagger spec to produce API client libraries from the codegen repo. They backported changes so the automatically-produced spec matches the one in the codegen repo.

All URIs in the generated client libraries are relative to `https://server.api.mailchimp.com/3.0`.

### How to Get the Full List of Endpoints

To get the **complete list of all endpoint paths**, you should download the `marketing.json` file from the official codegen repository:

```
https://github.com/mailchimp/mailchimp-client-lib-codegen/blob/main/spec/marketing.json
```

This JSON file contains the full OAS 2.0 Swagger specification with every path, operation, parameter, and schema definition for the Mailchimp Marketing API v3.0. You can parse the `"paths"` key from this JSON to enumerate all available endpoints.

Based on the search results, here is a comprehensive overview of the **Figma REST API Scopes**:

## Figma REST API Scopes

Scopes for personal access tokens and OAuth 2 tokens determine which endpoints can be accessed. The following table lists the scopes that are available.

Importantly, scopes do not supersede the permissions granted to you by an organization or the owner of a project, team, or file. You can only access files that you've created or that have been shared with you (whether shared directly, or because you belong to a corresponding project or team). Similarly, if you listed projects and teams, you'd only see the projects and teams that you can access in Figma's file browser.

### Available Scopes

Based on the various search results referencing the official documentation and API error messages, here is the full list of known scopes:

**File Content:**
- `file_content:read` — required for file endpoints like GET files, GET file nodes, GET images, etc.

**File Metadata:**
- `file_metadata:read` — required for file metadata endpoints.

**File Comments:**
- `file_comments:read` — for reading comments on files
- `file_comments:write` — for posting/modifying comments

**File Dev Resources:**
- `file_dev_resources:read` — for reading dev resources
- `file_dev_resources:write` — for creating/modifying dev resources

**File Code Connect:**
- `file_code_connect:read` — for reading code connect data
- `file_code_connect:write` — for writing code connect data

**File Variables (Enterprise only):**
- `file_variables:read` — only available to members of an org with an Enterprise plan
- `file_variables:write` — for modifying variables (Enterprise only)

**File Versions:**
- `file_versions:read` — for reading file version history

**Library:**
- `library_assets:read` — for reading library assets
- `library_content:read` — for reading library content

**Team Library Content:**
- `team_library_content:read` — for reading team library content

**Projects:**
- `projects:read` — for reading project data

**Current User:**
- `current_user:read` — for reading the current authenticated user's info

**Webhooks:**
- `webhooks:read` — required for GET webhook endpoints
- `webhooks:write` — required for creating, updating, and deleting webhooks

### Deprecated/Legacy Scopes

- `file_read` — deprecated for OAuth 2 tokens.
- `files:read` — no longer recommended and will be deprecated in the future. Instead, use a more specific scope like `file_content:read` or `file_comments:read`.

### Important Notes

- Legacy tokens (personal access tokens with no scopes or OAuth tokens with the `file_read` scope) will continue to work as-is. However, newer endpoints require additional scopes for your application to call them.

- For example, to read the content of files, you would grant the `file_content:read` scope to the OAuth app or personal access token.

- The token needs to have all the scopes needed to call all the endpoints to be used. For example, if you want to get projects in a team as well as get/modify variables in a file, the token should have scopes `files:read,file_variables:read,file_variables:write`.

For the most up-to-date and complete reference, visit the official documentation at: **https://developers.figma.com/docs/rest-api/scopes/**

## Zoom API OAuth Scopes Overview

Zoom provides documentation on OAuth scopes for internal apps, including classic scopes for existing applications and upgrade paths. Zoom also provides guidance on how to implement OAuth scopes for Zoom apps, including granular and optional scope configuration, authorization queries, and user permission management.

### Key Scopes You Mentioned

Here's a breakdown of the scopes you listed and their relevance:

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `meeting:read:admin` | Read access to all user meetings at the admin level |
| `user:read:admin` | Read access to all user information at the admin level |
| `recording:read` | Read access to cloud recordings |
| `webinar:read` | Read access to webinar data |
| `dashboard:read` | Read access to dashboard/metrics data |
| `report:read` | Read access to reports |

### Classic vs. Granular Scopes

Zoom has been transitioning from **classic scopes** (like the ones you listed) to more **granular scopes**. For example, the newer granular scopes break down `meeting:read:admin` into specific sub-scopes like `meeting:read:list_meetings:admin`, `meeting:read:meeting:admin`, `meeting:read:list_registrants:admin`, and `meeting:read:past_meeting:admin`.

### Adding Scopes to Your App

- When setting up a Server-to-Server OAuth app, you add scopes in your app's Scopes tab. For example, Zoom's starter API project recommends scopes like `meeting:write:admin`, `recording:write:admin`, `report:read:admin`, `user:write:admin`, and `webinar:write:admin`.

### Known Issues with Scopes

- Some developers on Zoom Workplace Business plans have reported that when creating a Server-to-Server OAuth app, only read-level scopes such as `meeting:read:admin` and `user:read:admin` appear, while write-level scopes never show up in the "Add Scopes" list.

- For dashboard-related scopes, the `GET /metrics/meetings` API requires scopes like `dashboard_meetings:read:admin` and `dashboard:read:admin`, but some developers have reported difficulty adding those scopes to their apps.

### Official Documentation Links

For the most comprehensive and up-to-date list of all available OAuth scopes, refer to:

1. **Internal Apps (Classic Scopes)**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/internal-apps/oauth-scopes/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/internal-apps/oauth-scopes/)
2. **Integrations (Classic Scopes)**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes/)
3. **OAuth Scopes Overview**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/integrations/oauth-scopes-overview/)
4. **API Reference**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/)

These official Zoom developer documentation pages will provide the full list of available scopes along with their descriptions and required API endpoints.

Here's what I found about the **Typeform API OAuth 2.0 scopes**:

## Overview

When you write your application to integrate with Typeform's APIs, you'll use OAuth 2.0 scopes to define the extent of your application's access to app users' data. This way, your app can request each user's permission to do specific actions on the user's behalf.

You define the scopes for your application during the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Grant retrieval stage. Your application's `https://api.typeform.com/oauth/authorize` link, including scopes, might look like this: `https://api.typeform.com/oauth/authorize?client_id={your_client_id}&redirect_uri=...&scope={scope1}+{scope2}+{scope3}`

## Complete List of Available Scopes

Based on the search results, the full list of Typeform OAuth 2.0 scopes includes:

| Scope | Description |
|---|---|
| **`offline`** | Enables refresh tokens for long-lived access |
| **`accounts:read`** | Read account/user information |
| **`forms:read`** | Read/retrieve typeforms |
| **`forms:write`** | Create, update, and delete typeforms |
| **`images:read`** | Read/retrieve images |
| **`images:write`** | Create and delete images |
| **`themes:read`** | Read/retrieve themes |
| **`themes:write`** | Create, update, and delete themes |
| **`responses:read`** | Read/retrieve form responses |
| **`responses:write`** | Write/manage form responses |
| **`webhooks:read`** | Read/retrieve webhooks |
| **`webhooks:write`** | Create, update, and delete webhooks |
| **`workspaces:read`** | Read/retrieve workspaces |
| **`workspaces:write`** | Create, update, and delete workspaces |

## Key Notes

- To receive a refresh token, your application must pass an extra `offline` scope during the authorization code request.

- If you're working with an OAuth token, make sure it has `accounts:read` scope to retrieve user information via the `/me` endpoint.

- When your app is up and running, users will see a consent screen that lists the scope of access for your app and prompts them to grant or deny access to your app.

- Scopes are separated by `+` signs in the authorization URL (e.g., `scope=forms:read+images:read+themes:read`).

For the official and most up-to-date documentation on scopes, visit: **https://www.typeform.com/developers/get-started/scopes/**

Here is a comprehensive overview of the **Calendly API v2 endpoints** based on the search results:

---

## Calendly API v2 — Endpoint Overview

### Authentication
The Calendly API v2 uses personal access tokens and OAuth 2.1 to authenticate requests. If you're integrating an internal application that only your team will use, authenticate with personal access tokens. If you're integrating a public application that many Calendly users will use, authenticate with OAuth.

### Access & Subscription Requirements
Developers can make GET and POST requests to API endpoints on behalf of a Calendly user on any subscription plan, including the Free plan (with the exception of a few endpoints that are specific to our Enterprise plan). For webhooks and Scheduling API endpoints, the Calendly user must have a paid subscription on the Professional, Standard, Teams, or Enterprise plan.

---

### Key API Endpoints

#### **Users**

- `GET /users/me` — Get your own info


#### **Organization Memberships**

- `GET /organization_memberships?organization={org_uri}` — All members of an organization


#### **Event Types**

- `GET /event_types?user={user_uri}` — All event types for a user
- `GET /event_types?organization={org_uri}` — All event types for an organization


#### **Scheduled Events**

- `GET /scheduled_events` — List events
- `POST /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/cancellation` — Cancel an event


#### **Event Invitees**
The webhook payload includes the invitee URI which can be used to make a GET request to the Get Event Invitee endpoint to expose additional invitee details.
- `GET /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/invitees` — List event invitees
- `GET /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/invitees/{invitee_uuid}` — Get a specific invitee

#### **Availability**

- `GET /event_type_available_times?event_type={uri}&start_time=...` — Available times for an event type
- `GET /user_busy_times?user={user_uri}&start_time=...` — User's busy times


#### **Scheduling Links**
Single use scheduling links (that haven't been used to book an event) expire after 90 days.
- `POST /scheduling_links` — Create a single-use scheduling link

#### **Webhook Subscriptions**
Make a POST request to the `/webhook_subscriptions` endpoint. Add your personal access token to authenticate the request. Set your server endpoint in the url value.
- `POST /webhook_subscriptions` — Create a webhook subscription
- `GET /webhook_subscriptions` — List webhook subscriptions
- `GET /webhook_subscriptions/{webhook_uuid}` — Get a specific webhook subscription
- `DELETE /webhook_subscriptions/{webhook_uuid}` — Delete a webhook subscription

When you create a webhook subscription, payloads will be sent in real time to your server endpoint when events are scheduled or canceled by either the host or the invitee. You can subscribe to `invitee.created`, `invitee.canceled`, or `routing_form_submission.created`.

#### **Scheduling API (New)**
With the new Scheduling API (Create Event Invitee endpoint), you can now schedule Calendly meetings via API — no redirects, iframes, or Calendly-hosted UI required — making it possible to enable AI assistants to book meetings conversationally, create custom booking workflows and scheduling automations, and build Calendly scheduling flows directly into your native apps, portals, or platform.
- `POST /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/invitees` — Create Event Invitee (schedule a meeting via API)

Calendly customers are required to be on a paid plan in order to access or use applications calling the Scheduling API.

---

### Additional Endpoints (Enterprise / Admin)
- `GET /organization_memberships` — List organization memberships
- `DELETE /organization_memberships/{uuid}` — Remove a member
- `GET /organization_invitations` — List org invitations
- `POST /organizations/{uuid}/invitations` — Invite a user to an organization
- `DELETE /organizations/{org_uuid}/invitations/{invitation_uuid}` — Revoke an invitation
- Invitee no-show management endpoints
- Data deletion/compliance endpoints (GDPR)

---

### Known Limitations
At this time, the API endpoints do not support setting or managing availability. To set availability, users will need to visit their Event Types page within their Calendly account.

At this time, the API endpoints do not support creating or managing event types. To manage an event or event type, users will need to visit their Event Types page within their Calendly account.

To cancel an event via the API, you can send a POST request to the cancel event endpoint. There is no API endpoint that supports rescheduling an event or invitee, however the cancel and reschedule URLs are on the invitee resource.

---

### Reference Links
- **API Reference**: [developer.calendly.com/api-docs](https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs)
- **Stoplight Docs**: [developer.calendly.com/api-docs/d7755e2f9e5fe-calendly-api](https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs/d7755e2f9e5fe-calendly-api)

- **Getting Started**: [developer.calendly.com/getting-started](https://developer.calendly.com/getting-started)

content.content:
# Calendly API v2 Endpoints from Source Document

Based on the provided web page content, here are the documented Calendly API v2 endpoints:

## User & Organization
- `GET /users/me` — Retrieve authenticated user information
- `GET /organization_memberships?organization={org_uri}` — List organization members

## Event Types
- `GET /event_types?user={user_uri}` — Retrieve user's event types
- `GET /event_types?organization={org_uri}` — Retrieve organization's event types

## Scheduled Events
- `GET /scheduled_events` — List booked appointments
- `POST /scheduled_events/{event_uuid}/cancellation` — Cancel a scheduled event

## Availability
- `GET /event_type_available_times?event_type={uri}&start_time=2024-01-20T00:00:00Z` — Query available time slots
- `GET /user_busy_times?user={user_uri}&start_time=2024-01-20T00:00:00Z` — Retrieve blocked time periods

## Webhooks
- `POST /webhook_subscriptions` — Register webhook for real-time notifications

## Limitation Notice
The article emphasizes that "you can't book appointments directly through the API" and "new event types must be created manually" in Calendly's interface, restricting write capabilities significantly.

**Note:** The source document does not provide a complete comprehensive list of all v2 endpoints—only these examples are explicitly documented.

# Typeform OAuth 2.0 Scopes

Based on the documentation, here are all available Typeform OAuth 2.0 scopes:

## Read Scopes

| Scope | Description | Endpoints |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| `accounts:read` | "Retrieve basic information from your Typeform account" | GET /me |
| `forms:read` | "Retrieve a list of forms from your Typeform account" | GET /forms, GET /forms/{form_id}, GET /forms/{form_id}/messages |
| `images:read` | Retrieve image data and descriptions | GET /images, /images/{image_id}, /images/{image_id}/image/{size}, /images/{image_id}/background/{size}, /images/{image_id}/choice/{image_size} |
| `themes:read` | "Retrieve a list of JSON descriptions for all themes" | GET /themes, GET /themes/{theme_id} |
| `responses:read` | "Return form responses and landing and submission information" | GET /forms/{form_id}/responses |
| `webhooks:read` | "Retrieve data about all the webhooks of a specific form" | GET /forms/{form_id}/webhooks, /webhooks/{tag}, /webhooks/{tag}/deliveries |
| `workspaces:read` | "Retrieve all workspaces in your account" | GET /workspaces, /workspaces/{workspace_id} |

## Write Scopes

| Scope | Description | Endpoints |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| `forms:write` | Create, update, delete forms and messages | POST/PUT/DELETE /forms, PUT /forms/{form_id}/messages |
| `images:write` | "Add images in your Typeform account" and delete | POST/DELETE /images |
| `themes:write` | Create, update, delete themes | POST/PUT/DELETE /themes |
| `responses:write` | "Delete form responses and landing and submission information" | DELETE /forms/{form_id}/responses |
| `webhooks:write` | Create, update, delete webhooks | PUT/DELETE /forms/{form_id}/webhooks/{tag} |
| `workspaces:write` | Create, update, delete workspaces | POST/PATCH/DELETE /workspaces |

## Special Scope

| Scope | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `offline` | Required to receive a refresh token |

# Zoom OAuth Scopes Overview

Based on the Zoom Developer documentation, here are the primary OAuth scope categories:

## **Account Scopes**
- `account:master` — "View and manage sub accounts"
- `account:read:admin` — "View account info"
- `account:write:admin` — "View and manage account info"

## **Billing Scopes**
- `billing:master` — "View and manage sub account's billing info"

## **Calendar Scopes**
- `calendar:read` — "View calendar"
- `calendar:read:admin` — "View calendar"
- `calendar:write` — "Manage calendar"
- `calendar:write:admin` — "Manage calendar"

## **Chat Scopes**
- `chat:read` — "View Chat Message Info"
- `chat:read:admin` — "View Chat Message Info"
- `chat:write` — "Manage Chat Message Info"
- `chat:write:admin` — "Manage Chat Message Info"
- `chat_channel:read` — "View current user's team chat channels"
- `chat_channel:read:admin` — "View all users' team chat channels"
- `chat_channel:write` — "View and manage current user's team chat channels"
- `chat_channel:write:admin` — "View and manage all users' team chat channels"
- `chat_message:read` — "View current user's team chat messages"
- `chat_message:read:admin` — "View all users' team chat messages"
- `chat_message:write` — "View and manage current user's team chat messages"
- `imchat:write` — "Send a team chat message to a Zoom Team Chat user or channel"

## **Clips Scopes**
- `clips:read` — "View your clips information"
- `clips:read:admin` — "View your clips information"
- `clips:write` — "Manage your clips information"
- `clips:write:admin` — "Manage your clips information"

## **Conference Room Connector (CRC) Scopes**
- `apiconnector:master` — Manage API Connector information
- `crc:master` — "View and manage sub account's CRC configuration"
- `crc_rooms:master` — Manage Cisco and Polycom rooms

## **Contact Center Scopes**
- `contact_center_attachment:read:admin` — "View your contact center attachment information"
- `contact_center_flow:write:admin` — Manage flows
- `contact_center_messaging:read:admin` — Access message history
- `contact_center_messaging:write:admin` — Send messages

content.content:
# Zoom OAuth Scopes - Remaining Categories

Based on the documentation provided, here are the additional scope categories available after Contact Center scopes:

## Contact Scopes
The contact scopes enable applications to manage contact information. These include `contact:read` for viewing contacts and `contact:write` for managing contact data.

## Dashboard Scopes
Dashboard-related scopes allow access to meeting and webinar analytics. The `dashboard:read:admin` scope provides viewing access to account-level dashboard data and reports.

## Group Scopes
Group management scopes include `group:read:admin` for viewing group information and `group:write:admin` for managing groups, members, and group settings.

## Meeting Scopes
Meeting scopes are extensive and include:
- `meeting:read` - View user's meeting information
- `meeting:write` - Manage user's meetings
- `meeting:read:admin` - View all users' meetings
- `meeting:write:admin` - Manage all users' meetings

These enable creation, updating, and deletion of meetings, as well as access to meeting details and participants.

## Phone Scopes
Phone-related scopes manage Zoom Phone functionality, including `phone:read:admin` and `phone:write:admin` for administrative access to phone settings and configurations.

## Recording Scopes
Recording scopes control access to recorded meetings:
- `recording:read` - View recordings
- `recording:write` - Manage recordings
- `recording:read:admin` - Access all recordings
- `recording:write:admin` - Manage all account recordings

## Report Scopes
Report scopes enable analytics access with `report:read:admin` providing viewing permissions for account usage reports and activity data.

## Role Scopes
Role management uses `role:read:admin` and `role:write:admin` for viewing and managing custom roles.

## User Scopes
User scopes include comprehensive user management:
- `user:read` - View profile information
- `user:write` - Manage account settings
- `user:read:admin` - View all users
- `user:write:admin` - Manage all users

## Webinar Scopes
Webinar scopes parallel meeting scopes and include `webinar:read`, `webinar:write`, `webinar:read:admin`, and `webinar:write:admin` for webinar management at user and account levels.

Here's a comprehensive overview of the **Zoom API v2** endpoints for meetings, users, webinars, recordings, groups, chat channels, and more:

## Zoom API v2 Overview

The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics.

### Base URL

The base URL is `api.zoom.us` followed by the version number of the API, currently at version 2. All requests must be sent via HTTPS; requests sent to HTTP will receive a 302 HTTP response code.

So the base is: **`https://api.zoom.us/v2/`**

### Key API Endpoint Categories & Paths

Based on the official documentation and references, here are the main endpoint paths:

---

#### **Users**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/users` | List users |
| POST | `/v2/users` | Create a user |
| GET | `/v2/users/{userId}` | Get a user |
| PATCH | `/v2/users/{userId}` | Update a user |
| DELETE | `/v2/users/{userId}` | Delete a user |
| GET | `/v2/users/{userId}/settings` | Get user settings |
| PATCH | `/v2/users/{userId}/settings` | Update user settings |
| GET | `/v2/users/{userId}/assistants` | List user assistants |
| POST | `/v2/users/{userId}/assistants` | Add assistants |

You can perform a GET on the address `https://api.zoom.us/v2/users` to list users.

---

#### **Meetings**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/users/{userId}/meetings` | List meetings |
| POST | `/v2/users/{userId}/meetings` | Create a meeting |
| GET | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}` | Get a meeting |
| PATCH | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}` | Update a meeting |
| DELETE | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}` | Delete a meeting |
| GET | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/registrants` | List meeting registrants |
| POST | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/registrants` | Add a meeting registrant |
| PUT | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/registrants/status` | Update registrant status |
| PUT | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/status` | Update meeting status |

The Zoom Meeting APIs allow developers to interface with Zoom Meetings and Webinars programmatically.

---

#### **Webinars**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/users/{userId}/webinars` | List webinars |
| POST | `/v2/users/{userId}/webinars` | Create a webinar |
| GET | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}` | Get a webinar |
| PATCH | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}` | Update a webinar |
| DELETE | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}` | Delete a webinar |
| GET | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}/registrants` | List webinar registrants |
| POST | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}/registrants` | Add a webinar registrant |
| PUT | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}/registrants/status` | Update registrant status |
| GET | `/v2/webinars/{webinarId}/panelists` | List webinar panelists |

---

#### **Cloud Recordings**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/users/{userId}/recordings` | List all recordings |
| GET | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings` | Get meeting recordings |
| DELETE | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings` | Delete meeting recordings |
| DELETE | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/{recordingId}` | Delete a specific recording |
| PUT | `/v2/meetings/{meetingId}/recordings/status` | Recover meeting recordings |

---

#### **Groups**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/groups` | List groups |
| POST | `/v2/groups` | Create a group |
| GET | `/v2/groups/{groupId}` | Get a group |
| PATCH | `/v2/groups/{groupId}` | Update a group |
| DELETE | `/v2/groups/{groupId}` | Delete a group |
| GET | `/v2/groups/{groupId}/members` | List group members |
| POST | `/v2/groups/{groupId}/members` | Add group members |
| DELETE | `/v2/groups/{groupId}/members/{memberId}` | Delete a group member |
| GET | `/v2/groups/{groupId}/settings` | Get group settings |
| PATCH | `/v2/groups/{groupId}/settings` | Update group settings |

---

#### **Chat Channels**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/chat/users/{userId}/channels` | List user's chat channels |
| POST | `/v2/chat/users/{userId}/channels` | Create a channel |
| GET | `/v2/chat/channels/{channelId}` | Get a channel |
| PATCH | `/v2/chat/channels/{channelId}` | Update a channel |
| DELETE | `/v2/chat/channels/{channelId}` | Delete a channel |
| GET | `/v2/chat/channels/{channelId}/members` | List channel members |
| POST | `/v2/chat/channels/{channelId}/members` | Invite members to channel |
| DELETE | `/v2/chat/channels/{channelId}/members/{memberId}` | Remove a member |
| GET | `/v2/chat/users/{userId}/messages` | List user's chat messages |
| POST | `/v2/chat/users/{userId}/messages` | Send a chat message |

The chat channels endpoint follows the pattern `https://api.zoom.us/v2/chat/users/userID/channels`.

---

#### **Accounts / Billing**
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v2/accounts` | List sub accounts |
| POST | `/v2/accounts` | Create a sub account |
| GET | `/v2/accounts/{accountId}` | Get sub account |
| GET | `/v2/accounts/{accountId}/billing` | Get billing info |
| PATCH | `/v2/accounts/{accountId}/billing` | Update billing info |
| GET | `/v2/accounts/{accountId}/plans` | Get plan info |
| POST | `/v2/accounts/{accountId}/plans` | Subscribe to plans |

---

### Additional Notes

- The Zoom API allows you to access all the basic functions available through the Zoom UI via HTTP requests. You can create, get, list, and update things such as meetings, users, rooms, groups, and many more.

- The API does not provide access to the in-meeting experience such as current attendee list, who is current speaker, or ability to mute participants; if you seek these features you should look at Zoom's SDKs.

- Zoom APIs enable developers to request information from Zoom, including (but not limited to) user details, meeting reports, dashboard data, as well as perform actions on the Zoom developer platform on a user's behalf.

### Official Documentation Links
- **Full API Reference**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/)
- **Chat API Reference**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/chat/methods/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/chat/methods/)
- **User API Reference**: [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/user/methods/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/user/methods/)
- **GitHub API Docs (v2)**: [github.com/zoom/api](https://github.com/zoom/api)

For the complete and most up-to-date list of all endpoints including parameters, request/response schemas, and scopes, I recommend checking the official Zoom Developer documentation linked above.

Here's what I found about the Zoom API v2 Dashboard/Metrics endpoints for meetings and webinars:

## Zoom API v2 — Dashboard / Metrics Endpoints

The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics.

### Base URL

The base URL is `api.zoom.us` followed by the version number of the API, currently at version 2. So all dashboard endpoints are prefixed with `https://api.zoom.us/v2/`.

### Key Dashboard (Metrics) Endpoints

1. **`/v2/metrics/meetings`** — This endpoint is used to retrieve meeting metrics data and differs from the reporting endpoint `v2/report/users/{user_id}/meetings`.

2. **`/v2/metrics/meetings/{meetingId}/participants`** — This endpoint is used to get meeting participant metrics, for example: `https://api.zoom.us/v2/accounts/{accountId}/metrics/meetings/{meetingId}/participants?type=past&page_size=300`.

3. **`/v2/metrics/meetings/{meetingId}/participants/qos`** — This endpoint lets you get a list of meeting participants from live or past meetings along with the quality of service they receive during the meeting such as connection quality for sending/receiving video, audio, and shared content.

### Query Parameters & Behavior

- If you do not provide the `type` query parameter, the default value will be set to `live` and thus, you will only see metrics for participants in a live meeting, if any meeting is currently being conducted.
- To view metrics on past meeting participants, provide the appropriate value for `type`. You can specify a monthly date range for the dashboard data using the `from` and `to` query parameters. The month should fall within the last six months.

### Scopes & Prerequisites

- The required scope is `dashboard_meetings:read:admin` and the rate limit label is **Heavy**.
- These endpoints are only available for paid accounts that have enabled the dashboard feature.

### Documentation Note

Some users on the Zoom Developer Forum have noted that documentation for the `/v2/metrics/meetings` and `/v2/metrics/meetings/{0}/participants` endpoints may be missing or hard to find in the current version of Zoom API docs.

### Official References

- **Zoom Developer Docs (API Reference):** [developers.zoom.us/docs/api/](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/) — Explore a wide variety of Zoom API endpoints and events to power your applications and integrations.
- **Zoom API v2 GitHub Repo:** [github.com/zoom/api](https://github.com/zoom/api) — The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics.

For the most up-to-date and detailed documentation on the webinar-specific metrics endpoints (e.g., `/v2/metrics/webinars`), I'd recommend checking the official [Zoom API Reference](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/) directly, as the structure mirrors the meetings metrics endpoints.
