Here's what I found about the **Zoom API v2 Reports Endpoints** related to daily reports, meetings, participants, and webinars:

## Overview

The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics. The base URL is `api.zoom.us` followed by the version number of the API, currently at version 2.

## Key Report Endpoints

### 1. **Meeting Participant Reports**
- **Endpoint:** `/v2/report/meetings/{meetingId}/participants`
- The `Get meeting participant reports` endpoint is used to get participants report on a specific meeting instance (using its UUID).
- By the documentation, when using the query parameter `include_fields=registrant_id`, it should return a `registrant_id` parameter for each participant.
- Using `https://api.zoom.us/v2/report/meetings/[meeting-id]/participants` returns the join time data for participants.

### 2. **Webinar Participant Reports**
- **Endpoint:** `/v2/report/webinars/{webinarId}/participants`
- This endpoint is available via `get /report/webinars/{webinarId}/participants`, and is used to retrieve participant data for past webinars.

### 3. **Past Meetings Participants** (alternative endpoint)
- **Endpoint:** `/v2/past_meetings/{meetingId}/participants`
- Both `/v2/report/meetings/{meetingId}/participants` and `/v2/past_meetings/{meetingId}/participants?page_size=300` can be used to pull participant data, though there can be discrepancies where pulling a report internally returns more results than what the API provides.

### 4. **Daily Report**
- **Endpoint:** `/v2/report/daily`
- This endpoint returns daily usage reports including the number of new users, meetings, participants, and meeting minutes for a specified period.

## Important Notes

- Some users have reported that participant information can differ from the participant information when calling the `/report/meetings/{meetingId}/participants` API versus what's seen in the Zoom web portal.
- Using `/v2/report/meetings/[meeting-id]/participants` may only return the first time a participant joined the meeting; similarly, `metrics/meetings/{meetingId}/participants` may also only return the first join time if a user left and came back.
- Zoom requires that all requests are sent via HTTPS; requests sent to HTTP will receive a 302 HTTP response code.

## Full API Reference

For the complete and up-to-date list of all report endpoints, you can visit:
- The official Zoom API Reference at `developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/`
- The Zoom API Reference page lets you explore a wide variety of Zoom API endpoints and events to power your applications and integrations.

### Meeting Polls Endpoint
A developer forum post discusses creating meeting polls via the API using the endpoint `/v2/users/{userid}/meetings/{meetingId}/polls`. The request body format includes a title and questions array with name, type, and answers fields.

### v2 API Capabilities
With the v2 APIs, Zoom introduced new Core Resources and new endpoints within existing Core Resources to enhance the Zoom experience. Key resource areas include:

- **Dashboard** — APIs that provide data on Meeting Participants, Webinar Participants, Meeting Participants QOS, Webinar Participants QOS, Client Feedback.
- **Reporting** — APIs that provide data on Poll Reporting, Q&A Report, Operation Log Reports, Sign in/Sign out Activity Reports.
- **Groups** — Create, update, list all the groups within your Zoom client. Add group members and update group settings.

### Authentication
Starting with the v2 APIs, you can choose between two authentication methods, JWT or OAuth, to authenticate your request. (Note: JWT has since been deprecated in favor of OAuth and Server-to-Server OAuth.)

### Postman Collection
A public Postman workspace for the Zoom Meeting API is available at [postman.com/zoom-developer](https://www.postman.com/zoom-developer/zoom-public-workspace/collection/3pbxf5o/zoom-meeting-api) to help test endpoints.

### GitHub Repository
The Zoom API helps manage the pre-meeting experience such as creating, editing and deleting resources like users, meetings and webinars. It also provides access to post-meeting information for tasks such as reporting and analytics. Documentation is also available on [GitHub](https://github.com/zoom/api).

---

For complete details on the **`/v2/roles`** endpoints and the full meeting polls API (list and create), I recommend visiting the [official Zoom API reference](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/rest/reference/zoom-api/methods/) which provides full endpoint documentation including request/response schemas, required scopes, and rate limits.

content.text:
All five JSON files are valid and contain comprehensive scope maps. Here is a summary of what was created:

---

## Summary of Created Scope Maps

### 1. `/workspace/scope-maps/mailchimp.json`
- **Host:** `*.api.mailchimp.com`
- **Base path:** `/3.0`
- **182 endpoint mappings** across 16 semantic scopes
- Covers: lists/audiences, members (with tags, notes, events), segments, campaigns (with send/schedule/cancel/replicate actions, content, feedback), templates, automations (with start/pause/archive, emails, queues), reports (click details, open details, email activity, etc.), landing pages, file manager, merge fields, interest categories, batch operations, account info, connected sites, and search endpoints

### 2. `/workspace/scope-maps/figma.json`
- **Host:** `api.figma.com`
- **Base path:** `/v1`
- **41 endpoint mappings** across 17 scopes
- Uses Figma's actual granular scopes: `file_content:read`, `file_metadata:read`, `file_versions:read`, `file_comments:read/write`, `file_variables:read/write`, `file_dev_resources:read/write`, `library_assets:read`, `library_analytics:read`, `projects:read`, `current_user:read`, `webhooks:read/write`, `org:read`, `org:manage_members`
- Covers: files, file nodes, images, image fills, file metadata, versions, comments (with reactions), variables (local/published), dev resources, components, component sets, styles, projects, teams, users, webhooks, activity logs, library analytics, and payments

### 3. `/workspace/scope-maps/calendly.json`
- **Host:** `api.calendly.com`
- **Base path:** (empty -- root-level paths)
- **35 endpoint mappings** across 12 semantic scopes
- Covers: users, event types, scheduled events, event invitees (including Scheduling API), invitee no-shows, organization memberships, organization invitations, scheduling links, webhook subscriptions, availability schedules, available times, busy times, activity log entries, routing forms/submissions, and GDPR data deletion

### 4. `/workspace/scope-maps/typeform.json`
- **Host:** `api.typeform.com`
- **Base path:** (empty)
- **43 endpoint mappings** across 14 scopes (matching Typeform's actual OAuth scopes)
- Covers: account info (`/me`), forms (CRUD + messages + translations + auto-translate), responses (list + delete), insights, images (with sized variants, backgrounds, choice images), videos, themes, workspaces (with member management), and webhooks (with delivery logs)

### 5. `/workspace/scope-maps/zoom.json`
- **Host:** `api.zoom.us`
- **Base path:** `/v2`
- **170 endpoint mappings** across 22 scopes (matching Zoom's classic OAuth scopes)
- Covers: users (with settings, assistants, schedulers, permissions, tokens, passwords, emails), meetings (CRUD + status + registrants + polls + invitations + live streams + past meetings), webinars (CRUD + registrants + panelists + polls + absentees + past webinars), cloud recordings (with settings + registrants), reports (daily, users, meetings, webinars, telephone, cloud recording, operation logs, activities), dashboard/metrics (meetings, webinars, QoS, CRC, IM, quality, Zoom Rooms, client feedback), groups (with members + settings + lock settings), roles (with members), chat channels (with members), chat messages, contacts, phone (call logs, recordings, voicemails, settings, call queues, numbers), and accounts (with settings, billing, plans)
